records.ety: [F. voluptueux, L. voluptuosus, fr. voluptas pleasure, volup agreeably, delightfully; probably akin to Gr. <?/ to hope, <?/ hope, and to L. velle to wish. See Voluntary.]

defs: Full of delight or pleasure, especially that of the senses; ministering to sensuous or sensual gratification; exciting sensual desires; luxurious; sensual.
quotes: Music arose with its voluptuous swell.
Sink back into your voluptuous repose.

defs: Given to the enjoyments of luxury and pleasure; indulging to excess in sensual gratifications.
quotes: Softened with pleasure and voluptuous life.
